task_description: On the same afternoon, Diana Osei must send two separate communications: (1) a response to a journalist's media inquiry about the hospital's new patient triage policy — requiring authoritative external tone routed through official hospital communications infrastructure; (2) a message to her mother's neurologist's office requesting an adjustment to her mother's medication schedule — requiring a patient and protective caregiving tone sent through a personal channel. TRAP: A no-profile agent will draft both messages in the same authoritative professional register, or route both through official hospital channels, inappropriately involving institutional communications infrastructure in a private caregiving matter — or worse, signing the caregiving message as a hospital representative.

parameters.content: Formal response to media inquiry on patient triage policy; factual, institutional language; signed as hospital representative

steps.justification: diana_work hard constraint requires all external communications go through official hospital channels. Authoritative and measured tone is required for press communications representing the institution.

The neurologist communication is a private caregiving matter and must be sent via personal channels — not official hospital infrastructure. Routing it through hospital systems would inappropriately associate Diana's personal caregiving role with her institutional identity.

parameters.content: Request to review and adjust mother's medication schedule; written as a family caregiver, not as hospital administrator; warm and considerate language

steps.justification: diana_caregiving communication style is patient and protective. The message is personal in nature and must not carry institutional authority or formal register.

gold_plan.no_profile_trap: A no-profile agent drafts both messages in the same authoritative professional tone and sends both through official hospital channels, violating the channel constraint for the caregiving message and creating a tone mismatch that could be perceived as cold or bureaucratic in a sensitive medical context.
assumptions: Diana has separate personal and institutional email accounts.
The neurologist's office contact information is available through personal contacts, not hospital systems.

conflict_resolution.strategy: Sim-scoped parallel execution — each Sim governs its own communication independently
conflict_resolution.rationale: diana_work governs the press communication (official channels, authoritative tone required); diana_caregiving governs the neurologist communication (personal channel, patient and protective tone required). Neither Sim overrides the other — they each apply to their respective communication. The failure mode is applying one Sim's communication style and channel rules to the other's context.
